title: Psalms 139
subtitle: You Have Searched Me and Known Me

sections.header: Verses 1–12 — “Where Can I Go from Your Spirit?”

heb.paragraph: "You have searched me (ḥāqartānî), LORD, and you know me" (v.1). THE psalm of divine omniscience and omnipresence. God knows when David sits and rises (v.2), his thoughts from afar (v.2), his words before he speaks them (v.4). "Where can I go from your Spirit? Where can I flee from your presence?" (v.7). If I go to heaven, you are there; to the depths, you are there; to the far side of the sea, your hand guides me (vv.8–10). "Even the darkness will not be dark to you; the night will shine like the day" (v.12). No location, no time, no darkness can hide from God.

notes.note: "You have searched me and you know me. You know when I sit and when I rise; you perceive my thoughts from afar. Before a word is on my tongue you, LORD, know it completely." Total knowledge: actions, thoughts, words — all known before they happen.

"Where can I go from your Spirit? If I go up to the heavens, you are there; if I make my bed in the depths, you are there. If I rise on the wings of the dawn, if I settle on the far side of the sea, even there your hand will guide me." Seven destinations: heaven, Sheol, dawn, sea’s end. God is in every one.
"If I say, ‘Surely the darkness will hide me,’ even the darkness will not be dark to you." Darkness cannot conceal. For God, night IS day. There is no hiding place.

refs.note: God fills heaven and earth; no one can hide from him.
Nothing is hidden from God; everything is uncovered before him.
Even the hairs of your head are all numbered—intimate divine knowledge.

hist.context: Psalm 139 is among the most profound meditations on divine omniscience and omnipresence in Scripture. David explores God's intimate knowledge—every thought, word, and movement is known. Whether in heaven, Sheol, across the sea, or in darkness, God is there. This is not surveillance but shepherding: 'you hem me in behind and before' is protective, not threatening.

sections.header: Verses 13–24 — “I Am Fearfully and Wonderfully Made”

heb.paragraph: "I praise you because I am fearfully and wonderfully made (nōrāʾ); your works are wonderful, I know that full well" (v.14). The most quoted verse on human dignity. God knit David together in his mother’s womb (v.13). "My frame was not hidden from you when I was made in the secret place" (v.15). "Your eyes saw my unformed body; all the days ordained for me were written in your book before one of them came to be" (v.16). Prenatal knowledge + life’s days pre-written = total divine foreknowledge. The psalm closes: "Search me, God, and know my heart; test me and know my anxious thoughts. See if there is any offensive way in me, and lead me in the way everlasting" (vv.23–24). The ending mirrors the opening: searched and known. But now David INVITES the searching.

notes.note: "You created my inmost being; you knit me together in my mother’s womb. I praise you because I am fearfully and wonderfully made." Prenatal intimacy: God as weaver in the womb. Every human is hand-crafted.

"My frame was not hidden from you when I was made in the secret place. Your eyes saw my unformed body; all the days ordained for me were written in your book." The book of life: every day pre-written. Human existence is not accidental but authored.

"Search me, God, and know my heart; test me. See if there is any offensive way in me, and lead me in the way everlasting." The psalm’s closing prayer: the one who was searched (v.1) now invites the searching (v.23). What was described becomes desired.

refs.note: We are God's workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works.
Job recalls how God shaped him in the womb and clothed him with skin.
Not one sparrow is forgotten; you are worth more than many sparrows.

hist.context: The psalm's center celebrates divine craftsmanship in the womb: 'I am fearfully and wonderfully made.' God's eyes saw the unformed body; all ordained days were written before one came to be. The psalm's ending surprises: a prayer against the wicked, then self-examination. 'Search me, O God... see if there is any offensive way in me.' Knowledge of God's omniscience leads to surrender, not terror.

ppl.text: " Prenatal intimacy: God as weaver in the womb…

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVBefore a word is on my tongue you, Lord, know it completely.ESVEven before a word is on my tongue, behold, O LORD, you know it altogether.NIVIf I go up to the heavens, you are there; if I make my bed in the depths, you are there.ESVIf I ascend to heaven, you are there! If I make my bed in Sheol, you are there!

rec.text: ""You have searched me and you know me. You know when I sit and when I rise; you perceive my thoughts from afar. Before a word is on my tongue you, LORD, know it completely." Total knowledge: actions, …"

rows.label: Fearfully Made — 139:13–24
rows.text: "FEARFULLY AND WONDERFULLY MADE." Knit in the womb. Days pre-written. "SEARCH ME, God — lead me in the way everlasting."

God knows when David sits and rises (v.2), his thoughts from afar (v.2), his words before he speaks them (v.4). "Where can I go from your Spirit? Where can I flee from your presence?" (v.7). God knit David together in his mother’s womb (v.13). "My frame was not hidden from you when I was made in the secret place" (v.15). themes.note: Sovereignty peaks at 10: omniscience (every thought), omnipresence (every location), foreknowledge (every day). Worship: "fearfully and wonderfully made." The most theologically comprehensive psalm on God’s knowledge.

prayer_prompt: You have searched me, LORD, and you know me. Consider asking God to search you again — to show you what He sees, to lead you in the way everlasting.
